req.title: External ACH transfer (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: ach_link verified

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-054 defines three key partitions: (1) valid transfers 1–10000 (EP positive), (2) transfers exceeding 10000 without step-up (NEG/422), and (3) transfers with insufficient funds (NEG/409). Boundary values at exactly 10000 and 10001 are critical. A state-transition case covers the account moving from sufficient-funds to insufficient-funds state mid-transfer. A decision-table case covers the step-up flag interaction with the limit. Together these seven tests provide full coverage of the stated error pathways and happy path.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queues_batch
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link is verified for the customer account
Account balance is >= 500.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 and a valid verified ACH destination
expected: Response body contains a non-empty transfer_id
trace: R-FIN-054: valid transfer up to $10,000 shall be queued as ACH batch
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queues_batch(auth_headers, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert body.get("transfer_id") not in (None, "")
    assert body["confirmation_sent"] is True

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_exceeds_limit_without_stepup_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link is verified for the customer account
No step-up verification token is present in the request
Account balance is >= 15000.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000.00 and no step_up_token field
expected: Response error code is STEP_UP_REQUIRED or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-054: transfers above $10,000 require step-up verification
R-FIN-054: error pathway 422 when amount > 10000 without step-up
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_exceeds_limit_without_stepup_returns_422(auth_headers, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 15000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "STEP_UP_REQUIRED" in body.get("error_code", "").upper() or \
           "step_up" in body.get("message", "").lower()

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_at_exact_limit_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link is verified for the customer account
Account balance is >= 10000.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.00 (exact upper boundary) and no step-up token
trace: R-FIN-054: transfers UP TO $10,000 are allowed without step-up
BVA: test value at the exact upper boundary of the permitted range
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_at_exact_limit_boundary_accepted(auth_headers, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 10000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert "step_up_required" not in body or body.get("step_up_required") is False

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link is verified for the customer account
No step-up verification token is present
Account balance is >= 10000.01
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 (one cent above upper boundary) and no step-up token
expected: Response indicates step-up verification is required
trace: BVA: test value immediately above the upper boundary triggers step-up path
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_returns_422(auth_headers, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 10000.01,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") or body.get("message"), \
        "Expected error detail in response body"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link is verified for the customer account
Account balance is less than the requested transfer amount (balance=10.00)
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 against an account with insufficient funds
trace: R-FIN-054: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_unverified_link_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link exists but is NOT verified (state=pending_verification)
Account balance is >= 500.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach referencing an unverified ACH account ID
expected: Response indicates ACH link is not authorised/verified
trace: R-FIN-054: precondition is ach_link verified; unverified link is an unauthorised error pathway
STC: ACH link state transition pending_verification -> active must complete before transfers allowed

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_stepup_present_above_limit_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
ACH link is verified
A valid step-up verification token has been obtained
Account balance is >= 12000.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=12000.00 AND a valid step_up_token in the request body
expected: Transfer ID is present in the response
trace: R-FIN-054: transfers above $10,000 require step-up verification — when step-up is supplied transfer shall succeed
DT: decision table row where amount>10000 AND step_up_token=valid => 202 queued
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_stepup_present_above_limit_accepted(
    auth_headers, verified_ach_account, valid_stepup_token
):
    payload = {
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 12000.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "step_up_token": valid_stepup_token
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert body.get("transfer_id") not in (None, "")
